2604.11246.title: Judge Like Human Examiners: A Weighted Importance Multi-Point Evaluation Framework for Generative Tasks with Long-form Answers
2604.11246.paper_summary: Evaluating the quality of model responses remains challenging in generative tasks with long-form answers, as the expected answers usually contain multiple semantically distinct yet complementary factors that should be factorized for fine-grained assessment. Recent evaluation methods resort to relying on either task-level rubrics or question-aware checklists. However, they still 1) struggle to assess whether a response is genuinely grounded in provided contexts; 2) fail to capture the heterogeneous importance of different aspects of reference answers. Inspired by human examiners, we propose a Weighted Importance Multi-Point Evaluation (WIMPE) framework, which factorizes each reference answer into weighted context-bound scoring points. Two complementary metrics, namely Weighted Point-wise Alignment (WPA) and Point-wise Conflict Penalty (PCP), are designed to measure the alignment and contradiction between model responses and reference answers. Extensive experiments on 10 generative tasks demonstrate that WIMPE achieves higher correlations with human annotations.
2604.11246.paper_summary_zh: 評估模型回應的質量在生成長篇回答的任務中仍然具有挑戰性，因為預期的答案通常包含多個語義上不同但互補的因素，這些因素應該被分解以進行細緻的評估。最近的評估方法依賴於任務級別的評分標準或問題意識檢查表。然而，它們仍然 1) 難以評估回應是否真正基於提供的上下文；2) 無法捕捉參考答案中不同方面的異質重要性。受到人類考官的啟發，我們提出了一個加權重要性多點評估（WIMPE）框架，該框架將每個參考答案分解為加權的上下文綁定評分點。設計了兩個互補的指標，即加權逐點對齊（WPA）和逐點衝突懲罰（PCP），用於測量模型回應與參考答案之間的對齊和矛盾。在10個生成任務上的廣泛實驗表明，WIMPE 與人類註釋之間的相關性更高。
2604.11246.author: Guoxin Yu et.al.
2604.11246.authors: Guoxin Yu, Chulun Zhou, Lemao Liu, Qi Wang, Mo Yu, Jialong Tang, Baosong Yang, Xiang Ao, Wao Lam, Yue Yu
